assertion.text: The largest distance of a single-logical-qubit stabilizer code at length 10, [[10,1,d]], is open within the band d in [3,4]; whether a [[10,1,4]] stabilizer code exists is undetermined here.

confidence.basis: agent-imported candidate claim; reviewer acceptance required

assertion.text: The [[4,2,2]] code with stabilizers XXXX, ZZZZ encodes 2 logical qubits in 4 physical qubits with distance 2, verified by exact recomputation of k and d.

confidence.basis: agent-imported candidate claim; reviewer acceptance required

assertion.text: The Steane code [[7,1,3]] (CSS from the [7,4,3] Hamming code) encodes 1 logical qubit in 7 physical qubits with distance 3, verified from its six stabilizer generators.

confidence.basis: agent-imported candidate claim; reviewer acceptance required

assertion.text: The perfect five-qubit code [[5,1,3]] (cyclic stabilizers XZZXI and rotations) encodes 1 logical qubit in 5 physical qubits with distance 3, and is optimal at its length.

confidence.basis: agent-imported candidate claim; reviewer acceptance required

assertion.text: The Shor code [[9,1,3]] (concatenated bit- and phase-flip repetition) encodes 1 logical qubit in 9 physical qubits with distance 3, verified from its eight stabilizer generators.

confidence.basis: agent-imported candidate claim; reviewer acceptance required
